text:
Wulf is on one island, I on another.
Fast is that island, by fen surrounded.
Slaughter-cruel men stalk on that island;
they will devour him, should he dare to come in a raiding troop.
Our fates are not the same.

For my Wulf I have waited, for his wide wanderings I have longed;
when the rainy weather came and I sat racked with sorrow,
then the battle-bold one bound me in his arms,
it was a joy to me, and yet it was a horror.

Wulf, my Wulf, your absence has made me sick,
your seldom-comings,
a mourning mind—not a lack of meat.

Do you hear, Eadwacer? Our wretched whelp
a wolf bears to the woods.

Torn apart with ease is that which was never together,
our song together.
